w:r.w:t: Integrated Water Leakage Reporting & Maintenance Management System
HydraNet – A Geo-Governed Digital System for Water Leakage Reporting and Maintenance Management
HydraNet is a structured, hierarchical, and privacy-conscious digital infrastructure designed to modernize water leakage reporting and maintenance operations. It functions as a
Smart Water Infrastructure Incident Reporting and Management System
, integrating public reporting with controlled operational execution and governance oversight.
Water leakages — including burst pipelines, distribution network failures, and surface infrastructure damage — result in:
Existing manual, fragmented, or non-integrated reporting systems restrict real-time visibility, delay intervention, and create administrative inefficiencies.
HydraNet addresses these operational gaps by introducing a structured, geo-governed, and role-based digital ecosystem that ensures:
Enable anonymous public reporting of water leakages
Establish a structured Water Utility and DMA governance framework
Digitize task assignment and repair approval workflows
Provide real-time monitoring and performance analytics
Ensure operational transparency, accountability, and efficiency
HydraNet operates under a clearly defined multi-tier administrative structure to ensure scalability, accountability, and controlled access.
The Administrator operates at the highest authority level and is responsible for:
Managing system configurations and GIS boundaries
Belongs to one Water Utility only
Has full oversight authority within their Water Utility
Monitoring performance across all DMAs within the Utility
Ensuring operational compliance and standards enforcement
The Utility Manager supervises DMA Managers but does not directly manage field teams.
Belongs to one DMA within a Water Utility
Is created and managed by the Utility Manager
Holds full operational authority within the assigned DMA
Reviewing, approving, or rejecting repair submissions
DMA Managers can only access data related to their assigned DMA.
Is managed by one DMA Manager
Branches cannot span across multiple DMAs or Utilities.
Teams cannot operate across multiple Branches.
Engineers cannot belong to multiple Teams simultaneously.
Is an Engineer designated by the DMA Manager
HydraNet applies GPS-based spatial containment logic to ensure accurate jurisdictional routing.
Each administrative layer is defined by geospatial boundaries:
Water Utilities are defined by approved geographic service areas.
DMAs are spatially defined within their respective Utility boundaries.
Upon report submission, the system automatically:
Identifies the corresponding DMA within that Utility.
Routes the report to the relevant DMA Manager.
If no matching Utility or DMA is detected:
The report is placed in an
The Administrator is notified for review and manual routing.
HydraNet consists of two integrated platforms supported by a centralized and secure backend infrastructure.
The public may report water leakages without registration.
Identify Utility and DMA using geospatial logic
Route report to the relevant DMA Manager
No personal information is collected from reporting users.
Upon receiving a notification, the DMA Manager will:
Assign the task to a Team within the DMA
The Team Leader coordinates field response
The Team Leader submits completion documentation including:
All actions are recorded in a comprehensive system audit log.
HydraNet enforces strict role-based access control:
Engineers & Team Leaders → Team-level operational access only
No cross-utility or cross-DMA data visibility is permitted.
HydraNet adheres to modern digital governance and data protection standards:
No personal data collection from public reporters
GPS collection strictly with user consent
Secure storage of reports and images
Reduce non-revenue water through faster response
Provide structured oversight across Utilities and DMAs
Reduce manual paperwork and tracking inefficiencies
Training of Administrative and Field Personnel
HydraNet establishes a structured, geo-governed, and hierarchically controlled digital ecosystem for water leakage management across Utilities and DMAs.
The system delivers a scalable, transparent, and accountable solution for modern water infrastructure management.
